narrative.headline: Engine and electrical gripes lead 2018 Malibu owner complaints
narrative.summary: Engine and electrical complaints dominate owner reports for this model, with powertrain and speed control issues also frequently noted. Owners most often describe check engine lights appearing shortly after purchase, along with episodes where the engine sticks at a fixed RPM and fails to accelerate normally. Some reports in multiple categories mention fires or crashes.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Owners report the check engine light illuminating during routine startup, sometimes within weeks of acquiring the vehicle. · 1 fire · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Owners report the check engine light coming on during startup, with some incidents occurring shortly after purchase. · 2 fire · 2 crash

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: Owners report the engine sticking at a fixed RPM with severely reduced power during acceleration, occurring both from a stop and while attempting to pass. · 3 crash · 1 injured

issues.title: Vehicle Speed Control
issues.blurb: Owners report the car holding a fixed RPM and making almost no power when trying to accelerate, happening from a stop or during passing maneuvers. · 3 crash · 2 injured

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 83 reports (12%), commonly about sputtering or uneven acceleration. Includes 1 crash and 1 injury; no fires reported.
